name: Cinnamon Swirl Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all ingredients in the bread machine pan.
2. Start the bread machine and set a kitchen timer for 30 minutes.
3. Cover a bread board with plastic wrap and dust with flour.
4. When the timer goes off, remove the dough from the pan using your hands, leaving the bread machine in place.
5. Place the dough on the floured board. Dust the top of the dough and your hands with flour.
6. Pat the dough into a 6" x 10" rectangle.
7. Combine 2 teaspoons cinnamon and 1 teaspoon sugar in a small bowl.
8. Spoon the mixture into three long rows down the center of the dough.
9. Roll the dough into a fat cylinder, rolling left to right, then fold it in half by twisting the ends together.
10. Place the rolled dough, with the ends down, back into the bread machine and close the lid.
11. Continue the cycle.
Comments:
- I used buttermilk powder mixed with water instead of actual buttermilk and didn't microwave it (it was already warm using lukewarm water).
- Used regular margarine and decreased the salt slightly.
- Added a bit more yeast as the dough rose well in my machine.
- The 30-minute timer is just a guide; wait until the machine finishes kneading and begins the first rise before removing the dough.
- If your machine has a preheat or rest phase, the countdown doesn't start until after that phase.
- Ensure the machine remains closed while applying cinnamon to avoid heat loss.
- Despite being warned against using much flour on hands and plastic wrap, I had to use significant amounts due to the dough's stickiness.
- The dough was very soft; it became "mooshy." Folding it as instructed proved difficult. After shaping, it simply slipped into the machine without needing to be placed elsewhere.
- Despite challenges, the final product was excellent and will be made again!
